title: Bytes, Object Representation, Alignment, Padding, and Endianness · Failure Case: Endianness Serialization
summary: A device protocol works on one architecture but leaks padding and faults on a stricter-alignment target. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem endianness serialization increment in portable binary representation codec. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Encode and decode external integers byte-by-byte or with defined conversion APIs instead of dumping native objects.
Measure rather than assume member offsets, tail padding, and layout changes across ABI targets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Endianness Serialization · Theory Endianness Serialization
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 8863. Produce bounded C evidence to encode values without copying native padding; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Encode and decode external integers byte-by-byte or with defined conversion APIs instead of dumping native objects.
